title: The least surprising chapter of the Manus story is what’s happening right now
description: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?

title: Mercor competitor Deccan AI raises $25M, sources experts from India
description: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.

title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
description: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
description: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

title: Melania Trump wants a robot to homeschool your child
description: The first lady sees AI and robotics playing a prominent role in the future of American education.

title: Bernie Sanders and AOC propose a ban on data center construction
description: Senator Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced companion legislation to halt construction on new data centers until Congress passes comprehensive AI regulation.

title: Google launches Lyria 3 Pro music generation model
description: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.

title: Reddit takes on the bots with new ‘human verification’ requirements for fishy behavior
description: Reddit will require suspected automated accounts to verify they’re human, as it ramps up efforts to curb bot-driven spam and manipulation.

title: Harvey confirms $11B valuation: Sequoia triples down
description: Investors like Sequoia, Andreessen Horowitz, Kleiner Perkins, and Elad Gil can't get enough of AI legal tech startup Harvey.

title: Granola raises $125M, hits $1.5B valuation as it expands from meeting notetaker to enterprise AI app
description: Granola's valuation jumped from $250 million to $1.5 billion with this round, and it has added more support for AI agents after users previously complained.

title: Meta launches new initiative to support entrepreneurship, drive AI adoption
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said in a memo to staff that small businesses have always been a big part of the company's business model, and that while tens of millions of entrepreneurs already use its platforms to grow and connect with customers, the company wants to do more in the space.

title: Meta turns to AI to make shopping easier on Instagram and Facebook
description: Meta is using generative AI to provide more product and brand information to consumers when they're shopping in its apps.

title: With Sift, two ex-SpaceX engineers are bringing the software that helped launch rockets to the factory floor
description: Sift is building the data infrastructure for advanced manufacturing.

title: Lucid Bots raises $20M to keep up with demand for its window-washing drones
description: Lucid Bots has seen demand accelerate over the last year for its window-cleaning drones and power-washing robots.

title: With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
description: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.

title: OpenAI’s Sora was the creepiest app on your phone — now it’s shutting down
description: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.

title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center
description: A "major artificial intelligence company" reportedly offered a Kentucky family $26 million to build a data center on their farm.

title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
description: Anthropic’s new auto mode for Claude Code lets AI execute tasks with fewer approvals, reflecting a broader shift toward more autonomous tools that balance speed with safety through built-in safeguards.

title: Spotify tests new tool to stop AI slop from being attributed to real artists
description: The idea behind the new tool is to give artists more control over which tracks are associated with their name on Spotify.

title: Databricks bought two startups to underpin its new AI security product
description: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.
